task: Write a Python function balanced(s) returning True when the parentheses in the string are balanced, considering only the characters '(' and ')' and ignoring everything else. A closing parenthesis must never appear before its opening partner: ')(' is not balanced. An empty string is balanced. Note one edge case it handles.
trace: The function uses a stack to keep track of the opening parentheses encountered. For every opening parenthesis, it gets pushed onto the stack. For every closing parenthesis, if the stack is empty or the top element of the stack is not an opening parenthesis, the function returns False. If the loop finishes without returning False, the function checks if the stack is empty, as an empty string is balanced. This approach is concise and easy to read while handling the edge case where the string contains no parentheses at all.
